input.job_description: Senior Python backend engineer. 5+ years Python, async/await, Postgres, FastAPI, AWS. Bonus: Kubernetes.
input.resume: Jane Doe. 7 years Python (Django + FastAPI). Built async microservices at TechCorp on AWS (ECS). Postgres + Redis. Open-source contributor.

interview_questions: Walk us through a complex async pattern you've debugged.
How do you decide between ECS and Kubernetes for a new service?
Describe a Postgres query you had to optimize at scale.
expected.rationale: Strong technical match — Python depth and FastAPI experience align tightly. Only gap is Kubernetes, which the JD lists as bonus, not required.

input.job_description: Frontend React engineer. 3+ years React, TypeScript, modern CSS. Bonus: design-system experience.
input.resume: Alex Smith. 8 years backend Java + Spring Boot. Some HTML/CSS in personal projects.

interview_questions: What drew you to apply for a frontend role?
Have you completed any React tutorials or side projects?
expected.rationale: Backend Java background with no professional frontend work — too far from the JD's React/TypeScript core. Polite pass; not a fit at this level.

input.job_description: Data Analyst at RetailMetrics Inc. Requires 3+ years SQL, Python (pandas, matplotlib), Tableau dashboards, and experience with e-commerce data. Bonus: dbt or Looker.
input.resume: Priya Sharma. 4 years SQL (Postgres, BigQuery). Python scripting with pandas for ETL. Built Tableau dashboards for sales reporting. No dbt experience. Background in retail operations analytics.

strengths: BigQuery and Postgres experience matches e-commerce data scale
Python/pandas for ETL aligns with role
interview_questions: Walk us through a Tableau dashboard you built end-to-end.
How have you handled data quality issues in ETL pipelines?
Are you familiar with dbt and would you be open to learning it?
expected.rationale: Solid SQL and Tableau foundation with relevant analytics background. Missing bonus tooling (dbt/Looker) but core skills match well enough to screen further.

input.job_description: DevOps Engineer at CloudNative LLC. Must have: Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS (EKS, RDS, S3), CI/CD with GitHub Actions, on-call rotation. Ideal: 3-5 years experience.
input.resume: Carlos Rivera. 6 years backend Node.js development. Used Docker containers in development. Deployed to Heroku. Basic AWS S3 familiarity from project work. No Kubernetes, Terraform, or CI/CD pipeline ownership.

strengths: Software engineering background provides debugging foundation
gaps: Heroku is not equivalent to AWS infrastructure
interview_questions: Have you used Docker beyond development environments?
What draws you from backend development into DevOps?
expected.rationale: Backend developer with minimal infrastructure exposure — missing all core DevOps requirements. Would need 18+ months of reskilling to be viable for this role.

input.job_description: VP of Product at FinTech startup Series B. 8+ years product management, experience managing PMs, roadmap ownership, stakeholder alignment with C-suite, fintech domain knowledge.
input.resume: Rachel Kim. 12 years in product management. Former CPO at two Series A startups. Led teams of 10+ PMs. Deep payments and lending domain knowledge. Board-level presentation experience.

strengths: 12 years PM experience well exceeds 8+ requirement
Deep fintech domain knowledge in payments and lending
Board-level presentation maps to C-suite alignment
gaps: CPO background may find VP of Product scope limiting — potential for overqualification mismatch
Series B stage may be earlier than past roles
interview_questions: Why are you interested in a VP vs. CPO title at this stage?
How do you approach building product culture in a high-growth fintech?
What's your experience with Series B company velocity vs. later-stage?
expected.rationale: Highly qualified on paper but potentially overqualified — risk of churn if expectations aren't aligned. Worth a call to assess motivation and fit for the stage.

input.job_description: Marketing Coordinator at GreenLeaf Agency. 1-2 years experience in marketing or communications. Proficiency in Canva, Mailchimp, basic social media scheduling (Hootsuite or Buffer). Strong writing skills.
input.resume: David Okafor. 5 years high school English teacher. Strong writing and communication skills. Ran school's Instagram account (1,200 followers). Created email newsletters for parent communications using Mailchimp. No agency or commercial marketing experience.

strengths: Strong writing skills verified by teaching background
Mailchimp experience is a direct match
Ran social media account with real audience
gaps: No commercial marketing or agency experience
Experience years exceed JD range but in wrong domain
No campaign performance or analytics exposure
interview_questions: Can you share examples of email campaigns you ran via Mailchimp?
How do you approach writing for different audiences?
Are you familiar with Canva or social scheduling tools?
expected.rationale: Career changer with transferable communication skills and real Mailchimp experience. Gap is commercial marketing context — worth a screen to assess coachability and tool ramp speed.

input.job_description: Junior Frontend Developer at Buildly. React, TypeScript, basic REST API integration. 0-2 years experience or relevant internships. Bonus: exposure to testing with Jest or Cypress.
input.resume: Mei Zhang. CS grad, University of Michigan. Two internships: frontend React at Shopify (4 months) and UI component work at Duo Security (3 months). Personal project: TypeScript React app with REST API calls deployed on Vercel. Familiar with Jest.

strengths: React and TypeScript match JD stack exactly
Two relevant internships exceed typical junior candidate experience
REST API integration demonstrated in personal project
gaps: Limited production scale experience beyond internships
interview_questions: Walk us through the TypeScript React project you deployed to Vercel.
How did you approach component testing with Jest in your internships?
Describe a tricky API integration problem you solved.
expected.rationale: Strong junior candidate — internship experience at reputable companies with stack alignment. Jest knowledge is a bonus match. Ready to advance to technical screen.

input.job_description: ML Engineer at Synthica AI. Requirements: Python, PyTorch, training and fine-tuning LLMs, experience with distributed training (FSDP or DeepSpeed), MLflow or W&B for experiment tracking. Publication record a plus.
input.resume: Andre Bastos. 4 years data science. Python (scikit-learn, XGBoost). Deployed classical ML models to production via Flask APIs. Familiar with TensorFlow from online course. No LLM fine-tuning. No distributed training experience. No papers published.

gaps: Classical ML background insufficient for deep learning engineering role
interview_questions: Have you worked with transformer-based models in any capacity?
Are you actively learning PyTorch or LLM fine-tuning?
expected.rationale: Classical ML background without the deep learning and LLM-specific skills the role requires. Missing PyTorch, distributed training, and experiment tracking tooling. Not a fit for this role today.
